meta.source_document: Engineering Architecture for AI-Driven Food and Supplement Intelligence (78pp)
meta.scope_note: BSIP2 packaged food engine only. Section 2 (Supplement Engine) explicitly excluded.
meta.label_constraint: Only signals observable or inferrable from packaged food labels are eligible for should_affect_score_now: true

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: NOVA 4 is a single bucket that fails to differentiate nutritionally balanced industrial foods from high-risk ultra-processed formulations. Siga subdivides NOVA 4 into C0.1, C0.2, C1-C3 using MUP counts and risk levels: MUP1 (nature-identical), MUP2 (artificially synthesized), and At-risk additives (EFSA/IARC-scrutinized). Composite nutritional thresholds apply: sugar >12.5g/100g, fat >17.5g/100g, salt >0.75g/100g.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — BSIP2 NOVA proxy collapses all Group 4 products into a single penalty tier, producing scoring errors for processed-but-nutritious products
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Requires MUP taxonomy dictionary and ingredient-to-tier mapping engine before scoring
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Over-penalising processed-but-nutritious foods if MUP1 (nature-identical) items are weighted equally to MUP2 (synthetic) items
primary_findings.notes: Prerequisite for EV-002. Implement taxonomy dictionary first, scoring second.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Siga's At-risk additive category enumerates additives under active EFSA or IARC safety evaluation. Counting them enables additive risk to scale with formulation severity rather than applying a binary additive-present/absent penalty. A product with 5 at-risk additives represents categorically different risk than one with 1.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — replaces the all-additive-equal penalty with a risk-weighted count
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Requires a maintained at-risk additive reference list mapped to Hebrew ingredient terms
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Regulatory lists change; a frozen v1 list risks penalising since-cleared additives or missing newly flagged ones
primary_findings.notes: The at-risk list is a maintenance obligation and must be versioned alongside BSIP2 releases.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Controlled human trial confirmed CMC (E466) reduces beneficial gut bacteria (Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, Akkermansia muciniphila) and SCFA production. P80 (E433) promotes pro-inflammatory Proteobacteria and thins the mucosal lining. Carrageenan (E407) disrupts tight junctions via ZO-1 proteins. Soy/sunflower lecithin (E322) shows minimal microbiota impact. Gum arabic serves as a bifidogenic prebiotic substrate.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Critical — current BSIP2 applies undifferentiated emulsifier penalties; this finding supports a three-tier model: high-risk (CMC, P80, carrageenan), neutral (lecithin), prebiotic (gum arabic)
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Ingredient text matching is sufficient; no nutrition panel data required
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Carrageenan food-grade vs degraded distinction unresolvable from labels — apply penalty uniformly per label presence
primary_findings.notes: Related to EV-019 (prebiotic gum reclassification). Implement as a tiered signal, not a binary additive flag.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: D-Allulose provides 0.4 kcal/g (not 4.0 kcal/g like fructose). ~70% is absorbed in the small intestine and excreted unchanged in urine. Does not elevate blood glucose or insulin. Not converted to glycogen. FDA has exempted it from the added sugar declaration requirement.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — treating allulose as standard sugar produces false high-sugar penalties on keto/low-sugar products legitimately formulated with allulose
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Ingredient list text match sufficient; apply 90% metabolic reduction to sugar score when allulose detected
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Allulose quantity not declared on Israeli labels; flag-only approach until quantity becomes detectable
primary_findings.notes: Long-term safety data limited but acute metabolic evidence is strong. Apply conservative partial credit only.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Sugar alcohols (sorbitol, xylitol, erythritol, maltitol) act osmotically above 10g/serving, drawing water into the large intestine and triggering laxation, bloating, and flatulence. EU/UK regulation mandates a laxative warning on products with >10% added polyol content. Erythritol has higher individual tolerance than other polyols.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — keto and low-carb products relying on polyol sweetening may exceed the safe threshold without visible consumer warning in Israel
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Detect polyol presence and count in ingredient list; flag when multiple polyols are present or product is keto/sugar-free positioned
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Cannot calculate exact grams from label alone; count polyol types and apply graduated risk, not a binary flag
primary_findings.notes: Erythritol should carry a lower risk weight than sorbitol/maltitol due to higher tolerance. Differentiate in risk weighting.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Viscous soluble fibers (psyllium, beta-glucan, guar gum) form a thick gel in the GI tract that delays gastric emptying and slows glucose diffusion, producing measurable postprandial glycemic reduction. Non-viscous soluble prebiotic fibers (inulin, FOS, polydextrose) undergo colonic fermentation but do not form gels and have minimal impact on glycemic kinetics. Treating them identically is a systematic scoring error.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Critical — current BSIP2 treats all dietary fiber identically. Inulin-fortified products score high-fiber but provide no glycemic dampening.
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Requires viscous fiber vocabulary dictionary to parse ingredient text reliably
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Exact viscous fiber quantity not on label; presence-only detection risks under-crediting psyllium-dominant products
primary_findings.notes: Implement vocabulary dictionary before modifying scoring. This finding is the scientific basis for fiber source differentiation.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Intrinsic fibers remain integrated within plant cellular structures with physical matrix intact. Isolated fibers (chicory inulin, pea fiber isolate, resistant maltodextrin) extracted and added to processed foods do not replicate the full systemic effects of whole-plant fiber. A structural discount of approximately 30-40% efficacy applies to isolated fiber sources.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — high-fiber claims on processed foods frequently rely on added isolated fibers that do not deliver equivalent metabolic protection
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Requires ingredient parsing to identify isolated fiber additions vs whole-grain intrinsic sources
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Ambiguous formulations can mix intrinsic and isolated fiber sources; cannot always distinguish without quantity data
primary_findings.notes: Apply 30-40% efficacy discount to detected isolated fiber sources in carbohydrate quality scoring.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Liquid food matrices bypass oral mechanoreceptors and fast-track gastric emptying, failing to activate stretch-sensitive vagal afferents that initiate PYY, GLP-1, and CCK-mediated satiety. This is mechanistically established. High-viscosity liquids can partially mimic solid-phase gastric retention but not fully.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — the beverage calorie density table partially captures this but the satiety dimension does not apply a liquid-state discount to protein drinks or meal replacement beverages
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Physical state is inferable from archetype routing and product name
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Viscous liquid products (yogurt, kefir, thick smoothies) should not receive the full liquid satiety penalty
primary_findings.notes: Apply graduated penalty: thin liquid > viscous liquid > semi-solid > solid. Archetype routing already handles most of this.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Intact plant cell walls insulate starch granules from pancreatic amylase, surviving cooking. Industrial milling destroys this insulation. Steel-cut oats and cracked grains retain protection; finely milled whole wheat flour does not, despite identical macronutrient content. The difference produces a substantially higher glycemic response for milled products.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — two products can have identical macronutrients but dramatically different glycemic impact based on grain processing state
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Requires ingredient text classification of grain processing state
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Whole grain marketing claims do not guarantee low milling disruption; whole grain flour is still fine-milled
primary_findings.notes: The matrix_integrity_framework already exists in BSIP2. This finding formally registers its scientific basis and extends it to grain processing state detection.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: High-shear extrusion uses extreme temperature, pressure, and shear to physically obliterate the natural food matrix, fully gelatinise starches, denature proteins, and produce hyper-bioaccessible, hyper-palatable products. Extruded grain shapes have a higher glycemic index than boiled whole grains with identical macronutrient composition. Extrusion parameters are rarely disclosed on retail packaging.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — cereal puffs, extruded snack shapes, and textured soy proteins require a matrix destruction penalty not fully captured by standard NOVA processing scores
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Extruded product signals detectable from ingredient terms and product name
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Apply penalty to refined-grain extrusion, not whole-grain puffed forms which retain fiber despite extrusion
primary_findings.notes: Related to EV-009 (intact grain). Extrusion is the most severe form of milling disruption.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: The sodium-to-potassium ratio is a stronger predictor of stroke and cardiovascular disease than sodium intake in isolation, due to the central role of the Na+/K+-ATPase pump in cellular electrolyte balance. A ratio <1.0 (more potassium than sodium, by mg) is protective. Processed foods are typically high-sodium and potassium-depleted.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Medium — current scoring penalises sodium in isolation; products with high sodium but higher potassium should receive reduced cardiovascular risk penalty
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Potassium not required on Israeli nutrition labels; population coverage <30%
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Missing potassium field must never be treated as zero; must explicitly handle null potassium
primary_findings.notes: Implement as a softening modifier on sodium penalty when potassium is present and Na:K < 1.0. When potassium absent, use sodium-only scoring unchanged.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratios are stronger cardiovascular disease predictors than absolute saturated fat content. The Food Compass lipid ratio method evaluates lipid class proportion rather than absolute SFA. Scoring absolute SFA penalises products rich in healthy unsaturated fats (olive oil, nut butters) when moderate SFA is co-present.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — the current fat dimension uses absolute saturated fat, which systematically mismeasures lipid quality in whole_food_fat category products
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Fat and saturated fat are standard label fields; unsaturated fat is computable as (total fat - saturated fat) from existing BSIP1 data
primary_findings.formula: unsaturated_fat_g = fat_g - fat_saturated_g; ratio = unsaturated_fat_g / fat_saturated_g
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Does not differentiate MUFA vs PUFA vs specific SFA chain lengths; this is an approximation, not a fatty acid profile
primary_findings.notes: This is computable TODAY from existing BSIP1 fields. Highest implementation priority among all EV findings.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: The industrial bliss point is the engineered ratio of sugar, salt, fat, and flavour enhancers (MSG, natural flavourings) that maximises palatability and overrides sensory-specific satiety, driving overconsumption. Products hitting this formulation window are associated with overconsumption in controlled settings regardless of macronutrient profile compliance. The exact quantification is proprietary and product-dependent.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — a product can score well on macronutrients while being specifically engineered for overconsumption
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Mathematical threshold calibration is product-dependent; requires research sprint before scoring
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Without calibrated thresholds, will falsely penalise naturally sweet+salty+fatty foods (dates, mixed nuts, cheese)
primary_findings.notes: Pre-condition: EV-001 (Siga MUP) and EV-002 (at-risk additive count) must be implemented first. Bliss point scoring should only activate on products with MUP > 0.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: In solid dairy matrices, free calcium ions react with liberated saturated fatty acids during small intestinal transit to form insoluble calcium soaps (saponification) that resist lipolytic absorption and are excreted. This attenuates postprandial lipemia in hard cheese vs butter or liquid milk with equivalent fat content. The matrix must be solid; the calcium-to-saturated fat ratio must be sufficient.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — standard fat scoring overestimates absorbable lipid load of hard cheeses. This is the scientific basis for the hard cheese governance exception already in Bari.
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Requires confirmed hard cheese sub-pool routing AND calcium data (not always on Israeli labels)
primary_findings.scope_limit: Hard cheese sub-pool ONLY. Must NOT apply to processed cheese, cream cheese, labaneh, or dairy spreads.
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Application to soft/processed cheese products would incorrectly reduce their fat penalty
primary_findings.notes: Governance Maturity Assessment v1 §4.2 already cleared Yellow Cheese for launch with DISTORTION-010 endemic note. This finding registers the saponification mechanism formally.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Fermentation activates endogenous phytases that dephosphorylate phytic acid (an anti-nutritional factor), significantly increasing bioaccessibility of Fe2+, Zn2+, and Mg2+. Fermented grains (sourdough, tempeh) also show reduced glycemic response due to organic acid production. The effect is time- and strain-dependent but directionally consistent across fermented products.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — already partially implemented in BSIP2 for sourdough bread. This finding formally grounds the fermentation bonus and extends it to all fermented categories.
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Fermentation markers already extracted by BSIP1 (extracted_fermentation_markers); scoring can use this signal now
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Sourdough flavour additives (vinegar, acidulants) are not genuine fermentation; detection must verify authentic fermentation markers not just flavour claims
primary_findings.notes: The sourdough D6 threshold problem (label data insufficient to confirm genuine sourdough) is documented in governance. This finding supports the bonus when authentic fermentation is confirmed.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Intrinsic nutrients are structurally bound within the food matrix with co-factors and phytochemicals affecting absorption. Synthetic fortification (added vitamins, mineral salts) can make processed foods appear nutritionally superior to whole foods on simple profiling grids. Bioavailability of synthetic fortification is compound-specific and generally lower than intrinsic forms. Source document recommends a 30% discount to detected synthetic fortifications.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: High — maps to DISTORTION-004 (fortification inflation) already registered in Bari governance. Provides the 30% discount evidence basis.
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Requires ingredient-level detection of synthetic fortification markers distinct from naturally occurring nutrients
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Some fortification compensates for genuine dietary deficiencies (iodised salt in low-iodine populations); apply discount to scoring but not consumer-facing disclosures
primary_findings.notes: DISTORTION-004 implementation is a BSIP3 debt item. This finding provides the evidence magnitude (30%) for when it is implemented.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Landmark human intervention studies show sucralose and saccharin can shift microbiome composition in a subset of responders, inducing impaired glucose tolerance. Steviol glycosides (stevia) and monk fruit (mogroside V) show better safety profiles. High inter-individual variability prevents population-level algorithmic penalisation; implement as a flag/disclosure, not a score deduction.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Medium — current BSIP2 treats all non-caloric sweeteners as neutral; a tiered risk classification is evidence-supported
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: High inter-individual variability prevents scoring application; implement as a consumer flag only
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Applying a score penalty for saccharin would unfairly disadvantage products where the risk is statistical, not individual
primary_findings.notes: Related to EV-004 (allulose) and EV-005 (polyols). Three sweetener findings together form a complete sweetener tier framework.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Reconstituting dried powders (milk powder, starch isolates, egg solids) back into liquid or semi-solid forms fails to re-establish the original cellular compartmentalisation. Reconstituted milk powder does not reform the protein-lipid microstructure of fresh milk. This produces accelerated enzymatic digestion similar to liquid matrices.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Medium — products listing reconstituted milk or milk powder as primary dairy source should receive a matrix quality downgrade relative to fresh dairy
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Reconstituted and milk powder are detectable by simple ingredient text matching
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Apply as disclosure flag + minor score adjustment, not a hard penalty; some high-quality products use milk powder legitimately
primary_findings.notes: Implementation is trivial and should be bundled with the dairy category pipeline run.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Gum arabic (E414), arabinogalactan, and acacia gum serve as fermentable fiber substrates promoting Bifidobacterium and Lactobacillus growth and pathogen inhibition. They do not degrade the gut mucosal lining. Penalising them as generic processing additives conflates them with genuinely harmful synthetic surfactants.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — EV-003 emulsifier penalty framework must explicitly exempt gum arabic and similar prebiotic gums
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Simple ingredient text exemption from emulsifier penalty; no calculation required
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Exemption from emulsifier penalty is correct; do not grant positive prebiotic credit without quantity data (excessive doses can cause bloating)
primary_findings.notes: Implement as part of the EV-003 emulsifier differentiation system.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Resistant starches (RS1-RS4) resist pancreatic amylase, reduce net glycemic carbohydrate load, and improve insulin economy. RS-rich ingredients include green banana flour, raw potato starch, and high-amylose corn starch. These should be partially subtracted from net glycemic carbohydrate calculations. RS3 (retrograded starch) requires thermal history confirmation not available from labels.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Medium — clean-label keto products using green banana flour are incorrectly penalised as high-carb
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: RS quantity not label-readable; RS3 cannot be confirmed from packaging
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: RS content changes with cooking history; static RS bonus without process confirmation would be inaccurate
primary_findings.notes: Implement as a flag + conservative partial credit pending better label data.

primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Plain, additive-free, live-culture dairy (yogurt + white cheese) MAY reach grade A, earned organically by score — in parallel to whole milk at 85/A. A plain live-culture yogurt is a whole-milk matrix PLUS a documented positive (fermentation, EV-015: phytase mineral bioaccessibility, organic-acid glycemic dampening, restructured protein), with no snack-bar-style irreducible compromise (sugar is intrinsic lactose, fat intrinsic dairy fat). It inherits the MILK precedent, not the snack-bar B-ceiling. B is the truthful ceiling for the sweetened/stabilized mainstream (~60% of the shelf), NOT for the category. The run_yogurt_003 0-grade-A result is mostly the EV-015 culture-detection gap (0/88 markers matched), not a punitive ceiling.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — governs whether an A may appear on the yogurt and white-cheese shelves; restoring EV-015 culture detection lifts the cleanest tier to ~80-86 -> A organically (~2-5 earned A's)
primary_findings.implementation_complexity: Low (no new rule — composes the EV-015 mechanism)
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Product co-sign satisfied 2026-06-01; A-condition C1-C6 still cannot activate until (a) EV-015 Israeli culture-vocabulary coverage is restored (currently 0%, precondition for C3) and (b) the A-threshold (80 vs 85) is reconciled before 139B publishes grades — both inherited by TASK-139B

a_eligibility_condition.C1_no_added_sugar: added_sugar_sources_count == 0; fruit-prep flavoring counts as added sugar; exclude the סוכרים->סוכר nutrition-text false positive
a_eligibility_condition.C2_no_engineered_additives: no modified starch (E-14xx), synthetic stabilizers/thickeners, artificial sweeteners, flavor enhancers, or colors; label-neutral natural gums not disqualifying (EV-019)
a_eligibility_condition.C3_live_culture_confirmed_and_credited: fermentation_marker_detected == true AND EV-015 bonus applied; requires the Gap 2 Israeli culture-vocabulary fix
a_eligibility_condition.C4_intact_dairy_matrix: milk base; milk-powder standardization permitted (NOVA <=3); reconstituted / חלב מאבקה-primary base NOT eligible (EV-018)
a_eligibility_condition.C6_verified_confidence: full ingredient + nutrition coverage; no confidence ceiling

primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: (1) Reading EV-014 hard-cheese saponification as a back-door A for processed/spreadable cheese — it is a fat-absorption exception, not an A-grant. (2) Crediting 'culture flavour' without microbial strain markers. (3) The סוכרים->סוכר nutrition-text false positive wrongly failing C1 on plain yogurts. (4) Restoring the manual shelf's blanket 5xA-by-format instead of earned A's.
primary_findings.published_grade_consequence: Yogurt A-count 5 (format-credited) -> ~2-5 (earned); category median 72 -> ~61-63. Flagged for Product co-sign.
primary_findings.notes: Governance ruling, not a new evidence finding. No new scoring rule/weight/threshold created; the A is earned via the already-registered EV-015 fermentation bonus. Anchored to frozen milk precedent (whole/4%/goat = 85/A) and frozen snack-bar B-ceiling (snk-001 = 70/B).

primary_findings.concept: Israeli Live-Culture Label Vocabulary — Extraction Coverage Restored (EV-015 input / EV-021 C3 precondition)
primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: EV-015 (fermentation bonus) reads extracted_fermentation_markers, but the BSIP1 enricher FERMENTATION_TERMS matched only תרבויות/ביפידובקטריום/לקטובציל. Real Shufersal yogurt labels declare the live-culture positive as חיידק פרוביוטי / חיידקי ביפידוס / BIFIDUS / חיידקי Bio / חיידקי יוגורט / תרבית, so run_yogurt_003 credited 0/88 markers — the category-defining positive was invisible to scoring. Extending the term set to the observed Israeli label vocabulary (non-interpretive, case-insensitive substring matching) raises detection to 49/88 has_live_cultures and 51/88 any fermentation marker. No new scoring rule, weight, or threshold; the bonus pathway is the already-registered EV-015 mechanism.
primary_findings.evidence_strength: Strong (direct label observation; run_yogurt_003 Shufersal corpus, 88 SKUs)
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — supplies the EV-015 input and satisfies EV-021 RULING-DAIRY-A-01 condition C3 (live_culture_confirmed_and_credited), previously blocked at 0% coverage.
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Extraction/label-observability fix, not a scoring rule. Downstream EV-015 bonus is already active, but no category is re-scored or published by this change; yogurt grade publication remains gated on TASK-139C (A-threshold reconciliation) and the live DEC-005 manual shelf is untouched.

label_observability.collision_audit: 0 new markers on any frozen/non-dairy corpus — run_001 snacks, run_bread_light_001, bread_retail_001/003, run_cereals_001, run_hummus_001, run_milk_001/002 all 0 hits. The single bread match (לחם הרים, מחמצת פרוביוטית) credits only the pre-existing מחמצת term — unchanged by this task.

guards_verified.golden_corpus_regression: 11 PASS / 1 WARN / 0 FAIL (run_regression_check.py). WARN = pre-existing anchor_soy_drink acceptable-secondary structural class; change-independent — the regression reads stored BSIP2 traces + structural_classifier.py and never invokes the BSIP1 enricher edited here.
guards_verified.enricher_unit_tests: 64/64 PASS (test_enricher.py), incl. NV-bar=0-markers and YOGURT_PLAIN unchanged.
guards_verified.frozen_invariants: Unmoved — milk run_004 (85/A), bread retail_003, snacks snk-001 (70/B): 0 new fermentation markers in their corpora; no re-score performed.

primary_findings.rollback: git revert of the FERMENTATION_TERMS block in 03_operations/bsip1/core/ingredient_enricher.py (plus the run_yogurt_003 entry in enrich_runner.py) restores prior 0/88 behavior; run_yogurt_003 BSIP1 output is non-authoritative evidence and re-enrichment is idempotent. Notify: Data Architecture (registry/observability) + Nutrition (EV-015/EV-021 owner).
primary_findings.source: TASK-139B — FERMENTATION_TERMS extension in 03_operations/bsip1/core/ingredient_enricher.py; re-run via enrich_runner.py --run run_yogurt_003.
primary_findings.notes: Implements the EV-021 C3 / Gap-2 Israeli culture-vocabulary fix. Does not itself publish grades or move frozen scores. Residual: חיידקי L.casei DN114-001 (1 DanActive-style SKU) not matched — Latin strain code outside task-named vocab; known minor gap.

primary_findings.concept: Grade-boundary reconciliation. The canonical `.claude/scoring.md` Grades table carried a stale 5-grade scale (A=85–100, no S-grade, every band shifted) that disagreed with the live engine `constants.py GRADE_THRESHOLDS` (6-grade: S≥90, A≥80, B≥65, C≥50, D≥35, E) and with the frozen milk run_004 artifact (A≥80; whole milk 85=A). Corrected the doc to the engine scale; A≥80 ratified as the dairy A cutoff. No engine/score change.
primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: The A-threshold ambiguity (80 vs 85) flagged in RULING-DAIRY-A-01 / EV-021 decided whether the cleanest plain/bio/lactose-free dairy (~80–86) reaches grade A. The live engine and the frozen milk run already compute A≥80 (six-grade S/A/B/C/D/E); only the scoring.md table was stale. Adopting A≥80 lets clean live-culture dairy reach A organically — the identical earned mechanism as whole milk at 85/A — satisfying EV-021 A-reachability with no category A-grant.
primary_findings.evidence_strength: Strong (direct: constants.py GRADE_THRESHOLDS + batch_run_milk_004 header both A≥80; frozen whole milk 85=A)
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — resolves the last RULING-DAIRY-A-01/EV-021 precondition (A-threshold) blocking yogurt/cheese grade publication (TASK-139 parent; TASK-142/143).
primary_findings.candidate_signal_name: (no new signal — grade boundary already in constants.py GRADE_THRESHOLDS)
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: No engine change. constants.py already = S≥90/A≥80/B≥65/C≥50/D≥35/E; this only corrects the stale scoring.md doc table to match. No category is re-scored or published by this entry; yogurt/cheese grade publication proceeds under the TASK-139 parent re-score with A≥80 confirmed.
primary_findings.label_observability: Not a label signal — a grade-band boundary. Authoritative source = constants.py GRADE_THRESHOLDS. After fix, scoring.md matches the engine across all bands: S 90–100 / A 80–89 / B 65–79 / C 50–64 / D 35–49 / E 0–34.
primary_findings.guards_verified: No code/scoring change → golden regression unaffected (reads constants.py, unchanged). Frozen invariants unmoved: whole milk 85→A (A≥80), snk-001 70→B (B≥65), bread retail_003 grades all identical before/after — the engine already used A≥80; only the doc was wrong. The hummus batch-report string 'A 85–100' is a separate stale display artifact (cosmetic, non-authoritative), flagged for optional cleanup.
primary_findings.rollback: `git revert` of the scoring.md grade-table edit restores the prior (stale) 5-grade table. No engine/score artifact touched; reversible with zero score impact.
primary_findings.source: TASK-139D (A-threshold reconciliation, folded out of closed TASK-139C). Basis: dairy_a_ceiling_ruling_139A.md §A-threshold; constants.py GRADE_THRESHOLDS; batch_run_milk_004 frozen header.
primary_findings.notes: Resolves the '80 vs 85' item RULING-DAIRY-A-01/EV-021 said 'must be reconciled before grades publish.' Outcome: A≥80 (six-grade scale) is authoritative; scoring.md corrected. Product co-signed ('Confirmed', 2026-06-01). Unblocks the TASK-139 parent closing re-score → TASK-142/143.

primary_findings.concept: Culture-credit propagation fix (BSIP1→BSIP2). TASK-139B extended the BSIP1 enricher FERMENTATION_TERMS to the real Shufersal live-culture label vocabulary, but the BSIP2 scorer derives has_fermentation from an INDEPENDENT list (signal_extractor.FERMENTATION_MARKERS_HE) and never reads the BSIP1 flag. Result: run_yogurt_003 detected 49/86 live-culture SKUs in BSIP1 yet credited 0/86 in the score (fermentation_bonus_applied=0; identical to the pre-139B distribution). The parent closing re-score surfaced this. Fix: mirrored 139B vocabulary into FERMENTATION_MARKERS_HE so the already-active fermentation bonus (R-02 direct + WFI ferm_bonus +5) sees real labels. Non-interpretive substring matching only; no new rule/weight/threshold/cap.
primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: RULING-DAIRY-A-01 C3 requires live culture confirmed AND credited. Detection without crediting leaves C3 structurally unsatisfiable. Mirroring the vocabulary into the scoring layer makes the existing fermentation positive earn its (already-defined) bonus on genuine live-culture dairy, the yogurt/cheese-defining matrix positive.
primary_findings.evidence_strength: Strong (direct: BSIP1 has_live_cultures=49/86 vs BSIP2 has_fermentation=3/86 pre-fix → 34/86 post-fix; fermentation_bonus_applied 0 → firing per WFI dimension notes).
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — the TASK-139 parent closing re-score of run_yogurt_003; gates TASK-142/143 dairy interpretation.
primary_findings.implementation_complexity: Trivial (term-list extension mirroring an approved sibling change).
primary_findings.recommended_action: implemented (TASK-139 parent re-score). REQUIRES Nutrition truthfulness sign-off (done) + Product co-sign for the published-candidate score movement (12 SKUs C→B).
primary_findings.candidate_signal_name: has_fermentation (existing) — vocabulary coverage extended, not a new signal
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Cultures are a label-observable, score-bearing positive already wired to R-02 / WFI. The fix only repairs vocabulary coverage so genuine live-culture dairy is credited; it manufactures no grade A (0 A before and after) — no category grant.
primary_findings.label_observability: Fully label-observable. Israeli retail live-culture vocabulary added to the BSIP2 scorer: תרבויות, חיידק פרוביוטי (+construct/plural forms), חיידקי ביפידוס/יוגורט/אצידופילוס, ביפידוס/bifidus, תרבית. Mirrors EV-022 (BSIP1 enricher).
primary_findings.guards_verified: (1) COLLISION AUDIT — 0 has_fermentation flips on every frozen/non-dairy corpus: milk_001/002 (milk_004 source), snacks run_001 (snk-001 source), bread_light_001, bread_retail_003 (262-file scan), cereals_001/002, hummus_001. (2) FROZEN ISOLATION (OLD-vs-NEW marker toggle, in-memory full-pipeline recompute): milk_004 top=85.0/A unchanged, snacks top=70.0/B (snk-001) unchanged, 0 SKUs moved by this patch. (3) Golden structural regression 11 PASS / 1 WARN (pre-existing anchor_soy_drink, change-independent — reads stored traces) / 0 FAIL. (4) Router regression 12/12 PASS.
primary_findings.rollback: Working-tree edit (uncommitted). Rollback = restore the FERMENTATION_MARKERS_HE block in signal_extractor.py (git checkout / revert the commit once made) → reverts has_fermentation to 3/86 and the distribution to B17/C44/D24/E1. run_yogurt_003 BSIP2 output is NON-AUTHORITATIVE evidence; re-score is idempotent. Notify: Data Architecture + Nutrition + Product.
primary_findings.source: TASK-139 parent closing re-score (Nutrition+Data). Files: signal_extractor.py FERMENTATION_MARKERS_HE; batch_run_yogurt_003.py; report run_yogurt_003_run_summary.json.
primary_findings.notes: RE-SCORE DELTA (run_yogurt_003, 86 SKUs): grade B 17→29, C 44→32, D 24 (=), E 1 (=); A 0→0 (UNCHANGED); median 55.7→56.1; ceiling 78.2→78.7/B. 12 live-culture SKUs C→B. TRUTHFUL 0-A FINDING (Nutrition sign-off): no SKU satisfies RULING-DAIRY-A-01 C1–C6 in this corpus — NOVA dist is 0×NOVA-1, 4×NOVA-2, 36×NOVA-3, 48×NOVA-4; the only 4 clean NOVA-2 plain/goat yogurts have EMPTY ingredient panels (C3 culture confirmation impossible) and are dimensionally capped 65–72/B by low nutrient_density (10–26) / protein_quality, not by any cap; all higher scorers are NOVA-3/4 engineered/sweetened (fail C1/C2). B/78.7 is the truthful ceiling for run_yogurt_003. This UPHOLDS 139A qualitative ruling (B truthful for mainstream; A reachable in principle, earned only) but contradicts its QUANTITATIVE ~2–5 earned-A estimate. OPEN (non-blocking, for Nutrition): milk goat=85/A vs goat-yogurt=66.7/B gap — is yogurt nutrient_density scored too harshly vs the milk table? Separate calibration question. RESIDUAL: BSIP1 49 vs BSIP2 34 has_fermentation — ~15 SKUs use culture vocab still unmirrored (all NOVA-3/4 sweetened → cannot reach A; non-blocking). REGISTRY: TASK-139B was recorded CLOSED on BSIP1-only detection; its score-crediting DoD was completed by this parent fix — Central Controller to decide bookkeeping (reopen 139B vs note in parent).

primary_findings.concept: Cream-cheese/spread router anchor (identity, not score) — fixes run_cheese_001 QA-CHS-001 47.4% misroute
primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: Cream cheese (high fat/low protein/flavored) lacks dairy signal strength; with no name anchor it fell to default/whole_food_fat. Four specific hard anchors (גבינת שמנת/ממרח גבינה/פילדלפיה/נפוליאון) restore dairy_protein identity so the dairy calorie table + R-04 cap relief apply and the EV-021 A-ceiling governs. Bare שמנת excluded; נפוליאון carries a cake exclusion.
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — unblocks TASK-142 misroute<5%/insufficient-0% DoD
primary_findings.implementation_complexity: Trivial (4 anchors + 1 exclusion; no scoring logic)
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Routing/identity change only — selects the category table; adds no scoring rule. Dairy A-ceiling still gates any A.
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Low — specific multi-word/brand anchors; bare שמנת excluded; נפוליאון cake-excluded; collision-audited vs maadanim/yogurt/milk (0 matches).
primary_findings.guards_verified: Router regression 15/15 PASS (12 frozen + 3 cream-cheese); collision audit 0 matches; determinism confirmed (run_001 re-run == run_002).
primary_findings.rollback: git revert router_v2.py cream-cheese hunk + נפוליאון exclusion + 3 corpus entries; restores 47.4% misroute. No score promoted.
primary_findings.notes: TASK-145, spun off TASK-142 QA-CHS-001. run_cheese_002: misroute 1.8%, insufficient 0, grades B23/A6/C27/D1; 6 macro-A white cheeses WITHHELD by A-ceiling C3 (no confirmed culture). NON-AUTHORITATIVE pending grade-publication sign-off.

primary_findings.concept: פילדלפיה anchor seasoning exclusion (identity, not score) — removes a non-cheese salt/spice blend from the cheese category (TASK-153)
primary_findings.scientific_rationale_short: The EV-025 פילדלפיה cream-cheese hard anchor (TASK-145) caught a non-cheese SEASONING blend (gtin 7290014217492, 'תערובת תיבול פילדלפיה', brand טעם וריח, Shufersal seasonings shelf 'מוצרי יסוד ותבלינים/תבלינים'; ingredients 'תבלינים ושמני תבלין,מלח,דקסטרוזה,...E551'; sodium 7,905 mg/100g ~16x the next-highest cheese) and routed it to dairy_protein/cream_cheese, displaying it LIVE at 60/C in the cheese comparison. The fix adds a label-observable name exclusion ('תערובת תיבול') to the פילדלפיה anchor — exactly mirroring the נפוליאון cake exclusion. The phrase 'תערובת תיבול' is exclusive to spice blends; real herbed Philadelphia cheeses ('פילדלפיה שום+ע.תיבול') lack 'תערובת' and still route to dairy_protein.
primary_findings.evidence_strength: Strong (the SKU is the sole sodium>1500 outlier in the 52-product cheese corpus; BSIP0 source_url = seasonings shelf; ingredients are spices/salt/dextrose/E551 with no dairy)
primary_findings.BSIP2_relevance: Direct — removes a category-identity defect from the live cheese page (TASK-152 Content review surfaced the '7,905 מ"ג' insight line)
primary_findings.implementation_complexity: Trivial (1 exclusion entry in ANCHOR_EXCLUSIONS; no scoring logic)
primary_findings.should_affect_score_now_reason: Routing/identity + curation change only — excludes a non-cheese from the category; adds no scoring weight/threshold/penalty. No published or frozen score changed; no other product's score moves (subtractive only).
primary_findings.risk_of_misuse: Low — exclusion is on the specific phrase 'תערובת תיבול' (seasoning-blend only); the 3 real cheeses containing bare 'תיבול' (incl. 'פילדלפיה שום+ע.תיבול') do NOT contain 'תערובת' and still route to dairy_protein (verified). Collision-checked vs frozen categories — none contain 'תערובת תיבול'.
primary_findings.guards_verified: Router regression 16/16 PASS (15 prior + new exclusion_philadelphia_seasoning negative case → routes to 'default', NOT dairy_protein); determinism confirmed (two consecutive runs byte-identical ignoring timestamp); frozen categories (milk/bread/snack/cereals/yogurt/maadanim) unmoved (their anchor cases unchanged & PASS); misroute remains <5% (the removed SKU was the misroute).
primary_findings.rollback: git revert the router_v2.py ANCHOR_EXCLUSIONS['פילדלפיה'] hunk + the exclusion_philadelphia_seasoning corpus entry → restores the pre-TASK-153 routing (seasoning re-enters cream_cheese). Also revert the cheese_frontend_v1.json product removal + _meta count (51->52), factory_run_003/frontend_package.json display flag/counts, and category_config.json displayable (51->52). No engine/score change to roll back. Notify: Data Architecture + Product + Content.
primary_findings.notes: TASK-153, spun off TASK-142/TASK-152. Curation: SKU removed from live cheese_frontend_v1.json (52->51 displayed, _meta.product_count/scored_count 51), factory_run_003/frontend_package.json (display_approved=false, withheld_seasoning_non_cheese=1, total scanned 59 unchanged), category_config.json (displayable 52->51, cream_cheese_spread pool 26->25). No other insight line referenced the removed SKU (its own '7,905 מ"ג' line went away with it). Engine UNMODIFIED.

guardrails.misconception: Thermodynamic equivalence of calories — all calories are metabolically identical
guardrails.bsip2_rule: BSIP2 must apply food matrix and satiety adjustments; calorie count alone is never the final score

guardrails.misconception: Inherent harm of all ultra-processed foods — NOVA 4 is uniformly bad
guardrails.bsip2_rule: BSIP2 must use Siga-style differentiation; fortified cereals and fermented dairy are not equivalent to confectionery

guardrails.misconception: Insoluble fiber is non-functional / merely a bulking agent
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Insoluble grain fiber has metabolic benefits (T2D protection, intestinal transit regulation); do not penalise it

guardrails.misconception: PDCAAS is the definitive protein quality measure
guardrails.bsip2_rule: PDCAAS over-estimates quality due to fecal nitrogen contamination from microbial activity; do not rely on PDCAAS-derived protein quality claims

guardrails.misconception: Natural sugars (honey, agave, dates) are metabolically superior to sucrose
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Once fructose/glucose enter the portal vein, metabolic pathways are identical; do not grant natural sugar sources a scoring exemption

guardrails.misconception: All food emulsifiers are uniformly toxic
guardrails.bsip2_rule: BSIP2 must differentiate synthetic surfactants (CMC, P80) from lecithins and prebiotic gums (see EV-003, EV-019)

guardrails.misconception: Organic certification guarantees micronutrient superiority
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Organic certification verifies pesticide avoidance, not vitamin/mineral concentration; do not award a scoring bonus for organic label alone

guardrails.misconception: Folic acid is harmful for MTHFR carriers
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Clinical evidence shows 400µg/day folic acid raises protective red blood cell folate across all MTHFR genotypes; do not penalise folic acid in BSIP2

guardrails.misconception: Liquid and solid calories are equally satiating per kcal
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Liquid matrices produce substantially lower satiety; the mechanism is established (see EV-008); apply liquid-state satiety discount

guardrails.misconception: Added isolated fiber dampens glycemic response equivalently to intrinsic plant fiber
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Isolated non-viscous fibers (inulin, chicory root) do not form the physical gel barrier required to slow glucose absorption (see EV-006, EV-007)

guardrails.misconception: All saturated fats carry equivalent cardiovascular risk
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Hard cheese calcium saponification (EV-014) and the fat ratio model (EV-012) directly contradict this; context-specific treatment required

guardrails.misconception: Non-caloric sweeteners have zero metabolic impact
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Sucralose and saccharin can induce dysbiosis-mediated glucose impairment in susceptible individuals (see EV-017); treat as flag-level risk

guardrails.misconception: Industrial heating always degrades nutritional value
guardrails.bsip2_rule: HPP and controlled heat increase carotenoid bioaccessibility; heating is not uniformly harmful

guardrails.misconception: High protein content on label equals high protein quality
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Limiting amino acids (e.g., lysine in wheat) cap the utility of all other amino acids; protein density is not protein quality

guardrails.misconception: Sodium and potassium should be evaluated in isolation
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Na:K ratio is the correct cardiovascular metric when potassium data is available (see EV-011)

guardrails.misconception: Allulose and fructose are metabolically equivalent
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Allulose is excreted largely unchanged and does not contribute glycemic load (see EV-004); must be excluded from sugar scoring

guardrails.misconception: Raw ingredients are inherently more satiating than processed ones
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Processing can concentrate viscous fibers or restructure protein to increase satiety; raw ≠ satiating by definition

guardrails.misconception: Any dietary carrageenan causes bowel inflammation
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Food-grade undegraded carrageenan at label doses is distinct from degraded poligeenan used in high-dose in vitro studies; do not use the colitis claim in consumer messaging

guardrails.misconception: All fortified minerals on a nutrition label are bioavailable equivalently
guardrails.bsip2_rule: Non-haem iron bioavailability is highly sensitive to phytate and polyphenol inhibition (see EV-016); fortified listing ≠ absorption guarantee

guardrails.misconception: A single category-free algorithm can fairly score all foods
guardrails.bsip2_rule: BSIP2 must apply category-specific calorie density tables, guardrails, and pool rules; universal scoring is constitutionally rejected by Bari governance

do_not_model_yet.domain: Synergistic multi-additive toxicity
do_not_model_yet.reason: Cumulative interactive effects across additive cocktails not characterized in humans

do_not_model_yet.domain: Individual gut microbiome emulsifier susceptibility
do_not_model_yet.reason: CMC/P80 degradation rate varies by individual microbiome baseline; population-level penalty justified but individual prediction is not

do_not_model_yet.domain: Quantitative satiety prediction for mixed meals
do_not_model_yet.reason: Complex multi-signal regulation; mathematical precision without physiological monitoring not achievable

do_not_model_yet.domain: Phytochemical bioavailability from commercial matrices
do_not_model_yet.reason: Flavonoid/polyphenol absorption is highly matrix-, co-nutrient-, and microbiota-dependent

do_not_model_yet.domain: Long-term cardiometabolic effects of mogrosides
do_not_model_yet.reason: Acute glycemic safety confirmed; long-term cohort data on insulin sensitivity and lipid profiles insufficient

do_not_model_yet.domain: Dietary AGE absorption kinetics
do_not_model_yet.reason: Proportion absorbed vs endogenously generated under varying metabolic states is debated

do_not_model_yet.domain: Microbial synthesis of Vitamin K2 from dietary fiber
do_not_model_yet.reason: Colonic menaquinone synthesis rate and systemic absorption not predictable from food label data

do_not_model_yet.domain: Interactive effects of pesticide residues and synthetic emulsifiers
do_not_model_yet.reason: No clinical evaluation in humans; theoretical risk only

do_not_model_yet.domain: Chronic toxicological limits of 3-MCPD and glycidyl esters
do_not_model_yet.reason: Established for infant formula; adult low-dose chronic impact under high uncertainty

do_not_model_yet.domain: Predictive modeling of postprandial insulin response from labels
do_not_model_yet.reason: Incretin-modulated insulin response is too individual to model from macronutrients alone

do_not_model_yet.domain: Subclinical epithelial permeability from occasional additive exposure
do_not_model_yet.reason: Chronic vs occasional exposure distinction not quantitatively established in humans

do_not_model_yet.domain: Comparative long-term efficacy of synthetic vs food-derived vitamin forms
do_not_model_yet.reason: High statistical confounding between vitamin isomers and synthetic salts in long-term trials

do_not_model_yet.domain: Comparative satiating capacity of fermentable vs viscous fibers
do_not_model_yet.reason: SCFA-mediated GLP-1 release from fermentable fiber is highly variable between individuals

do_not_model_yet.domain: Acrylamide variance within product batches
do_not_model_yet.reason: Acrylamide levels fluctuate dramatically within batches based on minor processing shifts; not predictable from ingredient label

do_not_model_yet.domain: Micronutrient bioavailability under varying dietary tannin loads
do_not_model_yet.reason: Inhibition of non-haem iron by coffee/tea tannins is meal-timing and gastric-pH dependent

do_not_model_yet.domain: Allulose-induced GLP-1 release thresholds in non-diabetic humans
do_not_model_yet.reason: Minimum effective oral dose for clinically relevant GLP-1 stimulation not established

do_not_model_yet.domain: Systemic accumulation of 4-methylimidazole (4-MEI)
do_not_model_yet.reason: Hepatic enzyme polymorphism variability prevents standardised toxicity calculation

do_not_model_yet.domain: Glycemic load modifiers in complex re-extruded multi-grain matrices
do_not_model_yet.reason: Starch recrystallisation in re-extruded grains varies dynamically with factory cooling cycles

do_not_model_yet.domain: Impact of food matrix destruction on peptide hormone translocation
do_not_model_yet.reason: PYY and GLP-1 signalling alteration under matrix destruction is highly individual

do_not_model_yet.domain: Calcium saponification efficiency in varying gastric pH
do_not_model_yet.reason: Saponification rate depends on gastric acid and pancreatic lipase secretion which vary significantly with age and health status

implement_now_label_ready.reason: Computed from existing BSIP1 fields today; no new data required
Ingredient text matching only; Hebrew vocabulary dictionary needed
BSIP1 fermentation markers already extracted; scoring activation only

research_further.gap: High inter-individual variability; implement as flag only

reject.reason: 3-MCPD levels require batch laboratory analysis not available from labels
Too individual; contradicts BSIP2 population-level model
Constitutionally rejected by Bari Governance v1

recommended_first_three_upgrades.title: Implement unsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio (EV-012)
recommended_first_three_upgrades.impact: High — corrects systematic mismeasurement of lipid quality in whole_food_fat category (tahini, nut butters) and dairy. Zero new data required: computable from fat_g and fat_saturated_g already in BSIP1.
recommended_first_three_upgrades.instruction: In BSIP2 fat dimension scoring: compute unsaturated_fat_g = fat_g - fat_saturated_g. Replace absolute saturated fat score with ratio-based score: protective bonus when ratio >= 2.0; risk penalty when ratio < 0.5. Preserve existing red-label sodium check independently.

recommended_first_three_upgrades.title: Implement emulsifier risk differentiation (EV-003 + EV-019)
recommended_first_three_upgrades.impact: High — replaces the undifferentiated emulsifier penalty with a three-tier model. Immediately corrects over-penalisation of lecithin-containing products and under-penalisation of CMC/P80-containing products.
recommended_first_three_upgrades.instruction: Build an emulsifier risk vocabulary in Hebrew. Tier 1 (high-risk: CMC/E466, P80/E433, carrageenan/E407): apply strong penalty. Tier 2 (neutral: soy lecithin/E322, sunflower lecithin): no penalty, no credit. Tier 3 (prebiotic gum: gum arabic/E414): exempt from all additive penalties. Flag output on product explanation as 'emulsifier risk level: high/neutral/prebiotic'.

recommended_first_three_upgrades.title: Activate fermentation quality bonus (EV-015)
recommended_first_three_upgrades.impact: Medium — fermentation markers are already extracted by BSIP1 (extracted_fermentation_markers). Scoring activation is a configuration change, not new engineering. Immediately credits sourdough, yogurt, kefir, and tempeh products that already have authentic fermentation signals detected.
recommended_first_three_upgrades.instruction: In BSIP2 scoring: when extracted_fermentation_markers is non-empty AND contains authentic fermentation signals (not just flavour acids), apply a fermentation bonus to the structural/mineral dimension. Bonus magnitude: +5 to +8 points on the relevant dimension depending on fermentation depth signal. Guard: fermentation bonus must not apply when fermentation_markers contains only 'vinegar' or 'lactic acid' without microbial strain signals.
